LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GEORGE POON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 501, THE HOUSING COURT, TO BE HEARD ON JUNE 8, 2013 AT 9:30 AM. MR. POON MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE COMPLAINT, MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 501, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 22, 2013 AT 4:00 PM PER LOCAL RULE 2.6B AND 2.6C. THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: RICHARD KRAMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

OPPOSITION TO MTN FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLT FILED BY DEFENDANT DAVIS, PETE DBA NORCAL REALTY

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF GEORGE KELLY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA'S LIBERAL POLICY TO DECIDE CASES ON THE MERITS MILITATES TOWARD GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND ABSENT A SHOWING OF PREJUDICE. DEFENDANTS FAILED TO SHOW PREJUDICE CAUSED BY GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT CONTENDS THAT NEITHER THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT NOR THE PROPOSED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUPPORTS A PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THIS CONTENTION IS PREMATURE AND IS NOT CONTEMPLATED ON A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND A COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEWING IT IF APPROPRIATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-05-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Set For Trial On May-19-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2013 Continued To Sep-25-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants' Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages In First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: Defendants George Poon and Pete Davis DBA Norcal Realty's Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages In First Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend to allege a high degree of probability of dangerous consequences and what those consequences are. Gawara v. Unites States Brass Corp. (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1341, 1361. Plaintiff shall file amended complaint within 10 days from the Notice of Entry of Order. Prevailing party to prepare order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damaes In First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs GEORGE POON and PETE DAVIS' Motion To Strike Prayer For Punitive Damages In First Amended Complaint is transferred to Department 501 to be heard on August 5, 2013, at 9:30 a.m. That department handles all landlord/tenant matters. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Continued To Aug-14-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Andrew E Westley of Law Offices of Andrew E Westely (415-362-2817) appear for plaintiff George Kelly moving party. No other appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF GEORGE KELLY's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is GRANTED. California's liberal policy to decide cases on the merits militates toward granting leave to amend absent a showing of prejudice. Defendants failed to show prejudice caused by granting leave to amend. Defendant contends that neither the original complaint nor the proposed first amended complaint supports a prayer for punitive damages. This contention is premature and is not contemplated on a motion for leave to amend a complaint. Defendant's motion to strike is off calendar in light of the amended complaint without prejudice to renewing it if appropriate. Order signed in open court. Judge Peter J. Busch, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GEORGE POON'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is transferred to Department 501, the Housing Court, to be heard on June 8, 2013 at 9:30 am. Mr. Poon must deliver courtesy copies of the Complaint, moving and reply papers to Department 501, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 22, 2013 at 4:00 pm per Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Richard Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-18-2013 Continued To Jun-12-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Apr-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Westley, Esq. (415-362-2817) for George Kelly, plaintiff and moving party / No appearances by defendant

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF GEORGE KELLY's Motion To Compel responses to requests for production documents is GRANTED. Defendant shall produce responsive documents and/or privilege log no later than May 13, 2013. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,400 to be paid within 30 days. Judge Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Westley, Esq., 415.362.2817, for plaintiff George Kelly; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GEORGE KELLY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF ANSWER TO COMPLAINT IS Granted. No opposition filed. The request for attorneys fees in the prayer for relief is stricken. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
